name: eschscholzia californica
mechanism: Isoquinoline alkaloids (including protopine-containing fractions) show GABAergic and other CNS-modulating sedative/anxiolytic activity in preclinical studies.
contraindications: Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Human evidence is limited but short-term use appears generally tolerated; drowsiness can occur, so avoid driving after use and avoid during pregnancy due to limited safety data.

sources.title: California poppy in anxiety and sleep formulations: clinical evidence review (Phytother Res.)
sources.note: Clinical context for combination products including Eschscholzia.

sources.title: Eschscholzia californica neuropharmacology study (J Ethnopharmacol. 2011)
sources.note: Mechanistic preclinical evidence for anxiolytic/sedative effects.

interactions: May potentiate: May increase sedation when combined with benzodiazepines
Caution with: Use caution with other CNS depressant botanicals due to additive drowsiness
therapeuticUses: traditional support for mild digestive comfort
sideEffects: mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or loose stool)
allergic rash or itching in sensitive individuals
